title: alsine setacea var. corymbulosa
intro: Alsine setacea var. corymbulosa, also known as Wood Sorrel, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Saxifragaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its astringent and diuretic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of tannins and oxalic acid. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including urinary problems, digestive issues, and skin conditions, due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Alsine setacea var. corymbulosa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
